summary: Technology executive who builds AI-augmented engineering organizations—teams, workflows, and operating models where governed LLM tooling multiplies what an engineering team can deliver without sacrificing quality or compliance. Currently leading platform engineering for a Healthcare SaaS serving 5,000+ urgent care providers while operationalizing AI-augmented delivery across a 27-person engineering organization. Leads from the front: personally evaluated and implemented AI tooling across code generation, review automation, and incident response before institutionalizing governance policy and team standards. 20+ years scaling engineering organizations and modernizing platforms across Healthcare SaaS, fintech, and enterprise software—consistently aligning technology strategy to measurable business outcomes.

experience.title: VP, Software Engineering and Product Development
bullets: ## Building the AI-Augmented Engineering Organization

Defined AI governance framework, security guardrails, and training programs; scaled AI-enabled workflows across delivery, code review, incident response, and knowledge retrieval—delivering ~25% faster cycle time, ~30% reduction in defect rework, and ~40% improvement in MTTR; transformed ad-hoc tool experiments into consistent, audited, and repeatable pipelines aligned with Security, Compliance, and Product.

Transformed engineering teams from ad-hoc AI tool users to agent-augmented reviewers—LLM tooling generates first-draft code, PR descriptions, test cases, and documentation; engineers review, refine, and merge with enforced quality gates. The team's constraint shifted from build capacity to review quality—a fundamentally better bottleneck.

Led from the front: personally evaluated AI tooling across code generation, PR review automation, knowledge retrieval, and incident response before institutionalizing governance frameworks and team standards—ensuring adoption policy was grounded in practitioner experience rather than delegated from a distance.
## Platform Modernization & Engineering Reliability

Led engineering for a Healthcare SaaS platform serving 5,000+ urgent care and emergency medicine providers; accountable for reliability, performance, and compliance (HIPAA, HL7, FHIR) across legacy and modern distributed systems.

Drove architectural modernization of mission-critical platforms, introducing CI/CD maturity, improved cloud observability, and AI-compressed incident response—decreasing initial triage times and consistently generating actionable post-incident learnings.
## Organizational Leadership & Strategic Operations

Built and led a multi-disciplinary engineering organization across multiple time zones (5 direct reports, 4 teams, 27 engineers); accountable for $4.8M in strategic engineering investments within a $23M R&D budget, delivering 35%+ improvement in team efficiency through process redesign and turning scattered knowledge into searchable, AI-curated portals.

Partnered cross-functionally with Product, Finance, and executive leadership to define and execute engineering roadmaps, shaping budget allocation decisions tied to AI tooling adoption, platform modernization, and team structure.

experience.title: Director, Enterprise Architecture & Customer Success
bullets: Architected and directed enterprise blockchain deployments for Fortune 100 pharmaceutical clients on the MediLedger platform, ensuring DSCSA track-and-trace compliance and supply chain integrity across regulated distribution networks.

Led a cross-functional team of implementation engineers and technical consultants; translated complex Kubernetes-based blockchain infrastructure into clear client delivery roadmaps, aligning platform capabilities to executive stakeholder objectives.

experience.title: Director, Solutions Group
bullets: Led an architecture team delivering multi-tier, cloud-native solutions for Fortune 500 enterprise clients across AWS and Azure, identifying emerging technology opportunities and translating them into actionable adoption strategies.

experience.title: Cloud & Enterprise Architecture Lead
bullets: Championed cloud-first modernization across multiple enterprise engagements—governing solution design, guiding infrastructure adoption, and mentoring engineering talent through hands-on architectural leadership.

experience.title: Director, Enterprise Solutions Architect
bullets: Architected enterprise integration patterns and governed technology adoption across a regulated financial services platform.
Partnered with CTO and executive leadership to communicate critical technical initiatives and scalable architectural solutions aligned to enterprise strategy.

experience.title: VP, Application Development Manager
bullets: Reduced support incident rework by 43% YoY by establishing an intake, definition, alignment, and assignment integrated process.
Built a hiring practice anchored in curiosity and continuous improvement — resulting in engineers who consistently performed within the top 10%.

experience.title: Enterprise Application Architect
bullets: Defined multi-platform roadmaps and annual planning cycles; delivered comprehensive enterprise solutions with full technical specifications while mentoring distributed engineering teams.

experience.title: Application Architect
bullets: Managed the software development team ensuring constant improvement, engagement, and productivity across delivery cycles.

items: OCI Certified — Cloud Infrastructure 2024 Generative AI Professional

aiPractitioner.title: AI-Augmented Engineering — Practitioner Record
aiPractitioner.intro: The AI governance framework and operating model changes above were built from hands-on practitioner work—personally evaluating and implementing AI tooling at the workflow level before defining governance policy, quality standards, and organizational change strategy.
bullets: Governance Architecture: Designed the engineering organization's AI adoption framework—defining LLM usage policies, no-auto-merge standards, code review quality gates for AI-generated output, security guardrails, and audit protocols satisfying HIPAA-adjacent Compliance requirements. Converted ad-hoc, personality-driven tool use into a governed, repeatable, audited practice across 4 engineering teams.

Operating Model Shift: Led the transition from manual code producers to agent-augmented reviewers—establishing structured PR quality criteria for AI-generated output and incremental rollout through low-risk wins before expanding to production workflows. Taught the team that the future of individual contribution is reviewing and directing agents, not writing every line.

AI-Compressed Incident Response: Deployed AI-assisted runbooks and knowledge retrieval pipelines that compressed initial triage times and automated structured post-incident learning generation—reducing on-call cognitive load and improving MTTR across distributed teams.

Practitioner Certification: OCI Certified — Cloud Infrastructure 2024 Generative AI Professional — hands-on credential covering prompt engineering, RAG architectures, fine-tuning, and responsible GenAI system design on cloud infrastructure. Used to inform architecture decisions, evaluate tooling from a practitioner standpoint, and lead AI enablement conversations with credibility.
